data.description:
## Why

Review-result sidecars (plan and code review) are pruned to the most recent `RETAIN_PER_TICKET = 10`
per ticket; older reviews are git-removed and unrecoverable. A ticket reviewed many times
(remediation loops, drift-refresh re-reviews) loses its earlier audit history, which contradicts the
goal of a navigable audit trail.

## What

Raise the review-sidecar retention cap from 10 to 50 and make it a single named constant governing
both the plan- and code-review prune paths. 50 is a bounded increase chosen to comfortably cover
realistic remediation-loop depth (observed re-review series are single-digit rounds, well under 50)
while still capping unbounded growth of `REVIEW_RESULT` events on the tickets branch (the hot path
for every write). It is a deliberate bound, not an unlimited retention.

## Scope

- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py` — raise `RETAIN_PER_TICKET` to 50; keep prune newest-first
  (ordering unchanged); only the retained count changes.
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py` — ensure the same cap governs the code-review prune (shared
  or mirrored constant), so both paths retain identically.

## Approach rationale

A single shared constant (rather than a new config key) keeps the change minimal and avoids
config-surface proliferation — no consumer has asked for a per-repo override, so YAGNI applies; a
config key can be added later if a real need appears.

## Compatibility & rollback

Purely a bound increase — no schema change, no format change. Already-pruned history is not
recovered (already deleted); going forward more is retained. Reverting to 10 simply prunes more
aggressively on the next write; no migration.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] After 50+ reviews on one ticket, up to 50 `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecars are retained and the write
      past 50 prunes the oldest. Oracle: a unit test appending N>50 synthetic review events and
      asserting the retained count is 50 and the newest are kept.
- [ ] The cap is one named constant governing both plan- and code-review prune paths. Oracle: a test
      asserting both prune paths honor the same value.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.
data.title: raise review-sidecar retention cap (10 -> 50, shared constant)
